name: argo-tugboat

metadata.name: argo-tugboat
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: Apache Software License
metadata.summary: Streamline your Argo Workflows with precision and confidence

documentation:
# Tugboat

> The tugboat guides the ship to flight, from harbor's hold to ocean's light.

Tugboat is a linter to streamline your [Argo Workflows](https://argoproj.github.io/workflows/) with precision and confidence.

```none
$ tugboat
whalesay.yaml:6:3: WF001 Invalid entrypoint

 4 |   generateName: test-
 5 | spec:
 6 |   entrypoint: ducksay
   |               ^^^^^^^
   |               └ WF001 at .spec.entrypoint in test-
 7 |   templates:
 8 |     - name: whalesay

   Entrypoint 'ducksay' is not defined in any template.
   Defined entrypoints: 'whalesay'.

   Do you mean: whalesay

Found 1 failures
```

variables.name: tugboat.analyzers.cron_workflow.MAX_CRON_WORKFLOW_NAME_LENGTH
documentation:
The maximum length of a CronWorkflow name.
Ref: https://github.com/argoproj/argo-workflows/blob/v3.5.6/workflow/validate/validate.go#L90-L93

functions.name: tugboat.analyzers.kubernetes.check_resource_name
documentation:
Check if the name is valid to be used as a Kubernetes resource name. This
function reports the following issues:

M009
   Resource name length error
M010
   Invalid resource name

See also
--------
Object Names and IDs
   https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/names/#names

functions.name: tugboat.analyze.analyze_yaml
documentation:
Analyze a YAML manifest and report diagnoses.

This function internally uses {py:func}`analyze_raw` to analyze the manifest.
The manifest is first parsed into a Python dictionary before being analyzed.

# Parameters

manifest

: The YAML manifest to analyze.

# Returns

list[Diagnosis]

: The diagnoses reported by the analyzers.

functions.name: tugboat.analyze.analyze_raw
documentation:
Analyze a raw manifest and report diagnoses.

This function underlyingly uses the plugin manager to run the analyzers
registered with the system. The diagnoses are collected and returned
as a list of Diagnosis objects.

Parameters
----------
manifest : dict
    The manifest to analyze.

Returns
-------
list[Diagnosis]
    The diagnoses reported by the analyzers.

functions.name: tugboat.analyze.is_kubernetes_manifest
documentation: Returns True if the dictionary *looks like* a Kubernetes manifest.

functions.name: tugboat.analyze.translate_pydantic_error
documentation: Translate a Pydantic error to a diagnosis object.

functions.name: tugboat.analyze._extract_expects
documentation:
Extract the expected values from the error message.

The expected values are like:

```none
"hello'", 'world' or 'hola'
```

functions.name: tugboat.analyze._guess_string_problems
documentation:
Guess the problems with the string input, return a list of suggestions.

Ref: https://ruudvanasseldonk.com/2023/01/11/the-yaml-document-from-hell

functions.name: tugboat.analyze._to_sexagesimal
documentation: Convert an integer to a sexagesimal string.

The augmented diagnosis reported by the analyzer.

This type extends the {py:class}`Diagnosis` and adds additional fields
to provide more context about the diagnosis.

modules.name: tugboat.parsers

This module provides the parser for Argo template syntax.

It parses the input text into an abstract syntax tree (AST) that can be used to
analyze the template and generate a report.

modules.name: tugboat.hookspecs
documentation:
{py:mod}`tugboat.hookspecs` manages the hook specifications for Tugboat.
It is used to define the hooks that can be implemented by plugins.

The hooks are defined as functions with specific signatures.
Tugboat calls these hooks during the linting process.

The Tugboat framework heavily relies on {py:mod}`pluggy` for hook management.
For details on how to implement hooks, please refer to the
[pluggy's documentation](https://pluggy.readthedocs.io/en/stable/).

functions.name: tugboat.utils.prepend_loc
documentation: Prepend the path to the location of each diagnosis in the iterable.

functions.name: tugboat.utils.join_items
documentation: Join items with a separator.

functions.name: tugboat.utils.get_context_name
documentation: Get the parent context name for a location.

methods.name: tugboat.parsers.ast.argo_template.Document.iter_references
documentation: Iterate over all references in this document.

methods.name: tugboat.parsers.ast.argo_template.SimpleReferenceTag.parse
documentation:
Parse a simple reference tag.

A typical lexemes sequence is like:

```python
[
    (0, Punctuation, "{{"),
    (2, Name.Variable, "foo"),
    (5, Punctuation, "."),
    (6, Name.Variable, "bar"),
    (9, Whitespace, "  "),
    (11, Punctuation, "}}"),
]
```

methods.name: tugboat.parsers.ast.argo_template.SimpleReferenceTag.format
documentation:
Create a string representation of the reference in the same format as
this reference tag type.

class_variables.name: tugboat.parsers.ast.argo_template.SimpleReferenceTag.raw
documentation: The raw text of the reference tag.

class_variables.name: tugboat.parsers.ast.argo_template.SimpleReferenceTag.reference
documentation: Reference to a variable.

classes.name: tugboat.parsers.ast.argo_template.ExpressionTag
documentation: This class recognizes the expression tag format and stores the literal content.

modules.name: tugboat.hookspecs.core
documentation:
This module defines the core hook specifications for Tugboat.

These hooks are used to extend the core functionality of Tugboat. Since the
standard Argo Workflows manifest formats are built-in, users may not need to
implement these hooks. However, these hooks are available for users who want to
extend Tugboat to support custom manifest formats or provide custom diagnoses.

functions.name: tugboat.hookspecs.core.parse_manifest
documentation:
Convert a raw manifest into a {py:class}`tugboat.schemas.Manifest` instance
if the format is recognized by the handler. Returns {py:obj}`None` if the
format is not recognized.

During this hook, the framework captures the {py:exc}`pydantic_core.ValidationError`
raised when parsing the manifest and converts it into a diagnosis. This allows
the handler to focus on the manifest structure and content, and leave the
validation to Pydantic and the framework.

# Parameters

manifest

: The manifest to parse.

# Returns

Manifest

: The parsed Manifest object if the format is recognized, otherwise {py:obj}`None`.

functions.name: tugboat.hookspecs.core.analyze
documentation:
Analyze the manifest and provide diagnoses.

This is the primary hook invoked during the linting process. The handler
should examine the manifest and return a list of diagnoses.

For Argo Workflows manifests, you can implement the hooks in the
{py:mod}`tugboat.hookspecs.workflow` module. These hooks are wrapped by
tugboat's built-in handlers, reducing the need to manage the workflow structure.

# Parameters

manifest

: The manifest to analyze.

# Returns

Iterable[Diagnosis]

: The diagnoses reported by the handler.

functions.name: tugboat.analyzers.template.check_field_references
documentation:
Check fields that may contains references.

Currently only part of the fields are checked. See the list below:

Implemented:

- `container`
  - `args`
  - `command`
  - `image`
  - `stdin`
  - `workingDir`

- `script`
  - `command`
  - `image`
  - `source`
  - `workingDir`

Intended excluded:

- `inputs` - handled in `check_input_parameters` and `check_input_artifacts` above
- `outputs` - handled in `check_output_parameters` and `check_output_artifacts` above
- `steps` - handled separately in another hook

modules.name: tugboat.hookspecs.workflow
documentation:
This module defines the hook specifications for Argo's [workflow] and
[workflow template] analysis.

[workflow]: https://argo-workflows.readthedocs.io/en/latest/workflow-concepts/#the-workflow
[workflow template]: https://argo-workflows.readthedocs.io/en/latest/workflow-templates/

functions.name: tugboat.hookspecs.workflow.analyze_workflow
documentation:
Analyze a workflow.

Parameters
----------
workflow : Workflow
    The workflow to analyze.

functions.name: tugboat.hookspecs.workflow.analyze_workflow_template
documentation:
Analyze a workflow template.

Parameters
----------
workflow_template : WorkflowTemplate
    The workflow template to analyze.

functions.name: tugboat.hookspecs.workflow.analyze_template
documentation:
Analyze a template.

This hook is called for each template in a workflow or workflow template,
but not including the 'templateDefaults'.

For issues reported by this hook, the `loc` attribute of the diagnosis
should start from the `template` object itself. The framework will manage
the outer structure and its location path.

# Parameters

template

: The template to analyze.

workflow

: The workflow that the template is part of.

functions.name: tugboat.hookspecs.workflow.analyze_step
documentation:
Analyze a workflow step.

For issues reported by this hook, the `loc` attribute of the diagnosis
should start from the `step` object itself. The framework will manage
the outer structure and its location path.

# Parameters

step

: The step to analyze.

template

: The template that the step is part of.

workflow

: The workflow that the template is part of.

classes.name: tugboat.schemas.manifest.Manifest

methods.name: tugboat.schemas.manifest.Manifest.name
documentation: Short-cut to the name of the manifest.

The base schema for the Kubernetes manifest.

This schema is generic and must be inherited with the `T_Spec` type
parameter, which is the schema for the {data}`spec` field of the manifest:

```python
class MyManifestSpec(BaseModel):
    ...

class MyManifest(Manifest[MyManifestSpec]):
    ...
```

modules.name: tugboat.constraints
documentation:
This module provides some generic constraints that can be used on linting models.

All functions in this module are generators that yield {py:class}`tugboat.Diagnosis`
objects when a constraint is not met. These functions can be used in analysis
hooks, yielding results using the {py:keyword}`yield from <yield>` syntax.

A typical usage of these functions is as follows:

```python
from tugboat import hookimpl
from tugboat.constraints import require_exactly_one

@hookimpl
def analyze_workflow(workflow: Workflow) -> Iterator[Diagnosis]:
    yield from require_exactly_one(
        model=workflow.metadata,
        loc=("metadata",),
        fields=["name", "generateName"],
    )
```

functions.name: tugboat.constraints.accept_none
documentation:
Check if all the specified fields are not set.

# Yield

{ref}`code.m005` for each unexpected field.

functions.name: tugboat.constraints.mutually_exclusive
documentation:
Ensures that at most one of the specified fields in the model is set, but
does not require any of them to be set.

# Yield

{ref}`code.m006` when more than one were set.

functions.name: tugboat.constraints.require_all
documentation:
Requires that all of the specified fields in the model are set.

# Yield

{ref}`code.m004` for any of the missing or empty fields.

functions.name: tugboat.constraints.require_exactly_one
documentation:
Requires that exactly one of the specified fields in the model is set.

# Yield

{ref}`code.m004` when none of the fields are set.
{ref}`code.m006` when more than one were set.

modules.name: tugboat.schemas
documentation:
{py:mod}`tugboat.schemas` contains classes representing the schemas of Argo Workflows.

All the listed classes are {py:class}`pydantic.BaseModel` classes used for input
data validation. These classes are immutable and do not allow any extra fields.
This ensures that the input data strictly follows the schema and that the data
model remains consistent throughout the program's execution, even when data is
transferred between different modules.

All the classes are derived from Argo Workflows' official [Field Reference]
documentation. If you find any discrepancies between the classes and the official
documentation, please report it as a bug.

Currently, many of the members are typed as {py:data}`typing.Any`.
This means that the field is not yet used in the codebase but is included in the
schema for future compatibility. This also provides the basic ability to validate
if there are any incompatible fields in the input data.

[field reference]: https://argo-workflows.readthedocs.io/en/latest/fields/

modules.name: tugboat
documentation:
{mod}`tugboat`, the main package of the Tugboat framework.

The Tugboat framework is a modular static analysis tool for software manifests.
It is designed to examine software manifests and report issues detected by the
analyzers.

This package exposes only the core components of the framework:

- {class}`Diagnosis`: The primary structure for a diagnosis.
- {data}`hookimpl`: The hook implementation marker for the Tugboat framework.

Other components are available in the subpackages of the framework but are not
automatically imported here; they are intended to be imported explicitly by
the user.

functions.name: tugboat._vendor.pygments.regexopt.regex_opt

Return a compiled regex that matches any string in the given list.

The strings to match must be literal strings, not regexes.  They will be

*prefix* and *suffix* are pre- and appended to the final regex.

modules.name: tugboat._vendor.pygments.util
documentation:
pygments.util
~~~~~~~~~~~~~

Utility functions.

:copyright: Copyright 2006-2024 by the Pygments team, see AUTHORS.
:license: BSD, see LICENSE for details.

functions.name: tugboat._vendor.pygments.util.get_choice_opt
documentation:
If the key `optname` from the dictionary is not in the sequence
`allowed`, raise an error, otherwise return it.

functions.name: tugboat._vendor.pygments.util.get_bool_opt
documentation:
Intuitively, this is `options.get(optname, default)`, but restricted to
Boolean value. The Booleans can be represented as string, in order to accept
Boolean value from the command line arguments. If the key `optname` is
present in the dictionary `options` and is not associated with a Boolean,
raise an `OptionError`. If it is absent, `default` is returned instead.

The valid string values for `True` are `1`, `yes`, `true` and
`on`, the ones for `False` are `0`, `no`, `false` and `off`
(matched case-insensitively).

functions.name: tugboat._vendor.pygments.util.get_int_opt
documentation: As {func}`get_bool_opt`, but interpret the value as an integer.

functions.name: tugboat._vendor.pygments.util.get_list_opt
documentation:
If the key `optname` from the dictionary `options` is a string,
split it at whitespace and return it. If it is already a list
or a tuple, it is returned as a list.

functions.name: tugboat._vendor.pygments.util.make_analysator
documentation: Return a static text analyser function that returns float values.

functions.name: tugboat._vendor.pygments.util.shebang_matches
documentation:
Check if the given regular expression matches the last part of the
shebang if one exists.

> ```pycon
> >>> from pygments.util import shebang_matches
> >>> shebang_matches('#!/usr/bin/env python', r'python(2\.\d)?')
> True
> >>> shebang_matches('#!/usr/bin/python2.4', r'python(2\.\d)?')
> True
> >>> shebang_matches('#!/usr/bin/python-ruby', r'python(2\.\d)?')
> False
> >>> shebang_matches('#!/usr/bin/python/ruby', r'python(2\.\d)?')
> False
> >>> shebang_matches('#!/usr/bin/startsomethingwith python',
> ...                 r'python(2\.\d)?')
> True
> ```

It also checks for common windows executable file extensions:

```
>>> shebang_matches('#!C:\\Python2.4\\Python.exe', r'python(2\.\d)?')
True
```

Parameters (`'-f'` or `'--foo'` are ignored so `'perl'` does
the same as `'perl -e'`)

Note that this method automatically searches the whole string (eg:
the regular expression is wrapped in `'^$'`)

functions.name: tugboat._vendor.pygments.util.doctype_matches
documentation:
Check if the doctype matches a regular expression (if present).

Note that this method only checks the first part of a DOCTYPE.
eg: 'html PUBLIC "-//W3C//DTD XHTML 1.0 Strict//EN"'

functions.name: tugboat._vendor.pygments.util.html_doctype_matches
documentation: Check if the file looks like it has a html doctype.

functions.name: tugboat._vendor.pygments.util.looks_like_xml
documentation: Check if a doctype exists or if we have some tags.

functions.name: tugboat._vendor.pygments.util.surrogatepair
documentation:
Given a unicode character code with length greater than 16 bits,
return the two 16 bit surrogate pair.

functions.name: tugboat._vendor.pygments.util.format_lines
documentation: Formats a sequence of strings for output.

functions.name: tugboat._vendor.pygments.util.duplicates_removed
documentation:
Returns a list with duplicates removed from the iterable `it`.

Order is preserved.

functions.name: tugboat._vendor.pygments.util.guess_decode
documentation:
Decode *text* with guessed encoding.

First try UTF-8; this should fail for non-UTF-8 encodings.
Then try the preferred locale encoding.
Fall back to latin-1, which always works.

functions.name: tugboat._vendor.pygments.util.guess_decode_from_terminal
documentation:
Decode *text* coming from terminal *term*.

First try the terminal encoding, if given.
Then try UTF-8.  Then try the preferred locale encoding.
Fall back to latin-1, which always works.

functions.name: tugboat._vendor.pygments.util.terminal_encoding
documentation: Return our best guess of encoding for the given *term*.

classes.name: tugboat._vendor.pygments.util.ClassNotFound
documentation: Raised if one of the lookup functions didn't find a matching class.

classes.name: tugboat._vendor.pygments.util.OptionError
documentation:
This exception will be raised by all option processing functions if
the type or value of the argument is not correct.

classes.name: tugboat._vendor.pygments.util.Future
documentation:
Generic class to defer some work.

Handled specially in RegexLexerMeta, to support regex string construction at
first use.

methods.name: tugboat._vendor.pygments.util.UnclosingTextIOWrapper.close
documentation: Don't close underlying buffer on destruction.

modules.name: tugboat._vendor.pygments.plugin
documentation:
# pygments.plugin

Pygments plugin interface.

lexer plugins:

```
[pygments.lexers]
yourlexer = yourmodule:YourLexer
```

formatter plugins:

```
[pygments.formatters]
yourformatter = yourformatter:YourFormatter
/.ext = yourformatter:YourFormatter
```

As you can see, you can define extensions for the formatter
with a leading slash.

syntax plugins:

```
[pygments.styles]
yourstyle = yourstyle:YourStyle
```

filter plugin:

```
[pygments.filter]
yourfilter = yourfilter:YourFilter
```

```
copyright

Copyright 2006-2024 by the Pygments team, see AUTHORS.

license

BSD, see LICENSE for details.
```

modules.name: tugboat._vendor.pygments.token
documentation:
pygments.token
~~~~~~~~~~~~~~

Basic token types and the standard tokens.

:copyright: Copyright 2006-2024 by the Pygments team, see AUTHORS.
:license: BSD, see LICENSE for details.

functions.name: tugboat._vendor.pygments.token.is_token_subtype
documentation:
Return True if `ttype` is a subtype of `other`.

exists for backwards compatibility. use `ttype in other` now.

functions.name: tugboat._vendor.pygments.token.string_to_tokentype
documentation:
Convert a string into a token type:

```
>>> string_to_token('String.Double')
Token.Literal.String.Double
>>> string_to_token('Token.Literal.Number')
Token.Literal.Number
>>> string_to_token('')
Token
```

Tokens that are already tokens are returned unchanged:

> ```pycon
> >>> string_to_token(String)
> Token.Literal.String
> ```

variables.name: tugboat.core.hookimpl
documentation: The hook implementation marker for the Tugboat framework.

classes.name: tugboat.core.Diagnosis

class_variables.name: tugboat.core.Diagnosis.type
documentation:
The diagnosis type.
When not provided, it defaults to "failure".

class_variables.name: tugboat.core.Diagnosis.code
documentation: Diagnosis code.

class_variables.name: tugboat.core.Diagnosis.loc
documentation:
The location of the issue occurrence within the manifest, specified in a
path-like format.

The first element is the key of the manifest, and the rest are the keys of
the nested dictionaries.

class_variables.name: tugboat.core.Diagnosis.summary
documentation:
The summary.
When not provided, the first sentence of the message will be used.

class_variables.name: tugboat.core.Diagnosis.msg
documentation:
The detailed message.

When multiple lines are used in the message, the framework will automatically
dedent it. This allows the analyzer to use Python multiline strings without
concern for indentation.

class_variables.name: tugboat.core.Diagnosis.input
documentation: The input that caused the issue.

class_variables.name: tugboat.core.Diagnosis.fix
documentation: The possible fix for the issue.

class_variables.name: tugboat.core.Diagnosis.ctx
documentation: The additional context.

A diagnosis reported by the analyzer.

This class serves as the fundamental structure for a diagnosis. It is used
to report issues identified by the analyzer. All analyzers must return a
diagnosis or a list of diagnoses when they are registered with the framework.

variables.name: tugboat.references.context.AnyStr
documentation: A special object that matches any string.

classes.name: tugboat.references.context._AnyStr
documentation: An object that matches any string.

methods.name: tugboat.references.context.ReferenceCollection.filter_unknown
documentation:
Filter out unknown references from the given references.

Parameters
----------
references : Iterable[tuple[_TN, _TR]]
    An iterable of tuples of nodes and references

Yields
------
tuple[_TN, _TR, _TR]
    A tuple of the node, the reference, and the closest valid reference.

methods.name: tugboat.references.context.ReferenceCollection.find_closest
documentation: Find the closest match for a given reference in a list of reference.

A {py:class}`set`-like collection of references, handling the special logic
of {py:data}`AnyStr`.

modules.name: tugboat.references
documentation:
Utilities for building available references within the given scope.

See Also
--------
Reference
   https://argo-workflows.readthedocs.io/en/latest/variables/#reference

functions.name: tugboat.references.workflow.get_global_context
documentation: Returns a context with the available global references.

functions.name: tugboat.references.workflow.get_workflow_context
documentation: Returns a context with the available references for the given workflow.

classes.name: tugboat.references.cache.cache
documentation:
Decorator to cache the results of a function.

This decorator is like {py:func}`functools.lru_cache`, but it returns a copy
of the cached value instead of the original value.

classes.name: tugboat.references.cache.LruDict
documentation: A dict that implements LRU cache

functions.name: tugboat.references.template.get_template_context
documentation: Returns a context with the available references for the given template.

functions.name: tugboat.console.utils.cached_read
documentation: Read the content of a file and cache it.

classes.name: tugboat.console.utils.VirtualPath
documentation:
A {py:class}`pathlib.Path`-like object that wraps a stream and mimics the
behavior of a file on disk.

It could be used to represent files that are read from standard input or
generated on the fly.

functions.name: tugboat.console.main.main
documentation:
Linter to streamline your Argo Workflows with precision and confidence.

This command performs static analysis on Argo Workflow manifests to catch
common issues and improve the quality of your workflows.

Examples:

 
  # Check all YAML files in the current directory
  tugboat

 
  # Check specific files
  tugboat my-workflow-1.yaml my-workflow-2.yaml

 
  # Analyze all YAML files in a directory
  tugboat my-workflows/

 
  # Read from stdin
  cat my-workflow.yaml | tugboat -

functions.name: tugboat.console.main.setup_logging
documentation:
Setup logging
We separate the configuration of the tugboat logger from the other loggers.

functions.name: tugboat.console.main.summarize
documentation:
Summarize the diagnoses.
Return a boolean indicating whether the checks passed or not.

functions.name: tugboat.console.outputs.console._calc_highlight_range
documentation: Calculate the range to highlight in the line.

modules.name: tugboat._vendor.pygments.filters
documentation:
pygments.filters
~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~

Module containing filter lookup functions and default
filters.

:copyright: Copyright 2006-2024 by the Pygments team, see AUTHORS.
:license: BSD, see LICENSE for details.

functions.name: tugboat._vendor.pygments.filters.find_filter_class
documentation: Lookup a filter by name. Return None if not found.

functions.name: tugboat._vendor.pygments.filters.get_filter_by_name
documentation:
Return an instantiated filter.

Options are passed to the filter initializer if wanted.
Raise a ClassNotFound if not found.

functions.name: tugboat._vendor.pygments.filters.get_all_filters
documentation: Return a generator of all filter names.

classes.name: tugboat._vendor.pygments.filters.CodeTagFilter
documentation:
Highlight special code tags in comments and docstrings.

Options accepted:

`codetags`

: A list of strings that are flagged as code tags. The default is to
  highlight `XXX`, `TODO`, `FIXME`, `BUG` and `NOTE`.

:::{versionchanged} 2.13 Now recognizes ``FIXME`` by default.
:::

classes.name: tugboat._vendor.pygments.filters.SymbolFilter
documentation:
Convert mathematical symbols such as \<longrightarrow> in Isabelle
or longrightarrow in LaTeX into Unicode characters.

This is mostly useful for HTML or console output when you want to
approximate the source rendering you'd see in an IDE.

Options accepted:

`lang`

: The symbol language. Must be one of `'isabelle'` or
  `'latex'`. The default is `'isabelle'`.

classes.name: tugboat._vendor.pygments.filters.KeywordCaseFilter
documentation:
Convert keywords to lowercase or uppercase or capitalize them, which
means first letter uppercase, rest lowercase.

This can be useful e.g. if you highlight Pascal code and want to adapt the
code to your styleguide.

Options accepted:

`case`

: The casing to convert keywords to. Must be one of `'lower'`,
  `'upper'` or `'capitalize'`. The default is `'lower'`.

classes.name: tugboat._vendor.pygments.filters.NameHighlightFilter
documentation:
Highlight a normal Name (and Name.\*) token with a different token type.

Example:

```
filter = NameHighlightFilter(
    names=['foo', 'bar', 'baz'],
    tokentype=Name.Function,
)
```

This would highlight the names "foo", "bar" and "baz"
as functions. `Name.Function` is the default token type.

Options accepted:

`names`

: A list of names that should be given the different token type.
  There is no default.

`tokentype`

: A token type or a string containing a token type name that is
  used for highlighting the strings in `names`. The default is
  `Name.Function`.

classes.name: tugboat._vendor.pygments.filters.RaiseOnErrorTokenFilter
documentation:
Raise an exception when the lexer generates an error token.

Options accepted:

`excclass`

: The exception class to raise.
  The default is `pygments.filters.ErrorToken`.

:::{versionadded} 0.8
:::

classes.name: tugboat._vendor.pygments.filters.VisibleWhitespaceFilter
documentation:
Convert tabs, newlines and/or spaces to visible characters.

Options accepted:

`spaces`

: If this is a one-character string, spaces will be replaces by this string.
  If it is another true value, spaces will be replaced by `·` (unicode
  MIDDLE DOT). If it is a false value, spaces will not be replaced. The
  default is `False`.

`tabs`

: The same as for `spaces`, but the default replacement character is `»`
  (unicode RIGHT-POINTING DOUBLE ANGLE QUOTATION MARK). The default value
  is `False`. Note: this will not work if the `tabsize` option for the
  lexer is nonzero, as tabs will already have been expanded then.

`tabsize`

: If tabs are to be replaced by this filter (see the `tabs` option), this
  is the total number of characters that a tab should be expanded to.
  The default is `8`.

`newlines`

: The same as for `spaces`, but the default replacement character is `¶`
  (unicode PILCROW SIGN). The default value is `False`.

`wstokentype`

: If true, give whitespace the special `Whitespace` token type. This allows
  styling the visible whitespace differently (e.g. greyed out), but it can
  disrupt background colors. The default is `True`.

:::{versionadded} 0.8
:::

classes.name: tugboat._vendor.pygments.filters.GobbleFilter
documentation:
Gobbles source code lines (eats initial characters).

This filter drops the first `n` characters off every line of code. This
may be useful when the source code fed to the lexer is indented by a fixed
amount of space that isn't desired in the output.

Options accepted:

`n`

: The number of characters to gobble.

:::{versionadded} 1.2
:::

classes.name: tugboat._vendor.pygments.filters.TokenMergeFilter
documentation:
Merges consecutive tokens with the same token type in the output
stream of a lexer.

:::{versionadded} 1.2
:::

functions.name: tugboat.console.outputs.junit.group_by_manifest
documentation:
Assume that the diagnoses are sorted by manifest. Returns groups of the
diagnoses by manifest.

modules.name: tugboat._vendor.pygments.lexer
documentation:
pygments.lexer
~~~~~~~~~~~~~~

Base lexer classes.

:copyright: Copyright 2006-2024 by the Pygments team, see AUTHORS.
:license: BSD, see LICENSE for details.

functions.name: tugboat._vendor.pygments.lexer.bygroups
documentation: Callback that yields multiple actions for each group in the match.

functions.name: tugboat._vendor.pygments.lexer.using
documentation:
Callback that processes the match with a different lexer.

The keyword arguments are forwarded to the lexer, except `state` which
is handled separately.

`state` specifies the state that the new lexer will start in, and can
be an enumerable such as ('root', 'inline', 'string') or a simple
string which is assumed to be on top of the root state.

Note: For that to work, `_other` must not be an `ExtendedRegexLexer`.

functions.name: tugboat._vendor.pygments.lexer.do_insertions
documentation:
Helper for lexers which must combine the results of several
sublexers.

`insertions` is a list of `(index, itokens)` pairs.
Each `itokens` iterable should be inserted at position
`index` into the token stream given by the `tokens`
argument.

The result is a combined token stream.

TODO: clean up the code here.

variables.name: tugboat._vendor.pygments.lexer.inherit
documentation: pylint: disable=invalid-name

classes.name: tugboat._vendor.pygments.lexer.LexerMeta
documentation:
This metaclass automagically converts `analyse_text` methods into
static methods which always return float values.

classes.name: tugboat._vendor.pygments.lexer.Lexer

methods.name: tugboat._vendor.pygments.lexer.Lexer.__init__
documentation:
This constructor takes arbitrary options as keyword arguments.
Every subclass must first process its own options and then call
the `Lexer` constructor, since it processes the basic
options like `stripnl`.

An example looks like this:

```python
def __init__(self, **options):
    self.compress = options.get('compress', '')
    Lexer.__init__(self, **options)
```

As these options must all be specifiable as strings (due to the
command line usage), there are various utility functions
available to help with that, see [Utilities].

methods.name: tugboat._vendor.pygments.lexer.Lexer.add_filter
documentation: Add a new stream filter to this lexer.

methods.name: tugboat._vendor.pygments.lexer.Lexer.analyse_text
documentation:
A static method which is called for lexer guessing.

It should analyse the text and return a float in the range
from `0.0` to `1.0`. If it returns `0.0`, the lexer
will not be selected as the most probable one, if it returns
`1.0`, it will be selected immediately. This is used by
`guess_lexer`.

The `LexerMeta` metaclass automatically wraps this function so
that it works like a static method (no `self` or `cls`
parameter) and the return value is automatically converted to
`float`. If the return value is an object that is boolean `False`
it's the same as if the return values was `0.0`.

methods.name: tugboat._vendor.pygments.lexer.Lexer._preprocess_lexer_input
documentation: Apply preprocessing such as decoding the input, removing BOM and normalizing newlines.

methods.name: tugboat._vendor.pygments.lexer.Lexer.get_tokens
documentation:
This method is the basic interface of a lexer. It is called by
the `highlight()` function. It must process the text and return an
iterable of `(tokentype, value)` pairs from `text`.

Normally, you don't need to override this method. The default
implementation processes the options recognized by all lexers
(`stripnl`, `stripall` and so on), and then yields all tokens
from `get_tokens_unprocessed()`, with the `index` dropped.

If `unfiltered` is set to `True`, the filtering mechanism is
bypassed even if filters are defined.

methods.name: tugboat._vendor.pygments.lexer.Lexer.get_tokens_unprocessed
documentation:
This method should process the text and return an iterable of
`(index, tokentype, value)` tuples where `index` is the starting
position of the token within the input text.

It must be overridden by subclasses. It is recommended to
implement it as a generator to maximize effectiveness.

Lexer for a specific language.

See also {doc}`lexerdevelopment`, a high-level guide to writing
lexers.

Lexer classes have attributes used for choosing the most appropriate
lexer based on various criteria.

```
.. autoattribute:: name
   :no-value:
```

```
.. autoattribute:: aliases
   :no-value:
```

```
.. autoattribute:: filenames
   :no-value:
```

```
.. autoattribute:: alias_filenames
```

```
.. autoattribute:: mimetypes
   :no-value:
```

```
.. autoattribute:: priority
```

Lexers included in Pygments should have two additional attributes:

```
.. autoattribute:: url
   :no-value:
```

```
.. autoattribute:: version_added
   :no-value:
```

Lexers included in Pygments may have additional attributes:

```
.. autoattribute:: _example
   :no-value:
```

You can pass options to the constructor. The basic options recognized
by all lexers and processed by the base `Lexer` class are:

`stripnl`

: Strip leading and trailing newlines from the input (default: True).

`stripall`

: Strip all leading and trailing whitespace from the input
  (default: False).

`ensurenl`

: Make sure that the input ends with a newline (default: True). This
  is required for some lexers that consume input linewise.

  :::{versionadded} 1.3
  :::

`tabsize`

: If given and greater than 0, expand tabs in the input (default: 0).

`encoding`

: If given, must be an encoding name. This encoding will be used to
  convert the input string to Unicode, if it is not already a Unicode
  string (default: `'guess'`, which uses a simple UTF-8 / Locale /
  Latin1 detection. Can also be `'chardet'` to use the chardet
  library, if it is installed.

`inencoding`

: Overrides the `encoding` if given.

classes.name: tugboat._vendor.pygments.lexer.DelegatingLexer
documentation:
This lexer takes two lexer as arguments. A root lexer and
a language lexer. First everything is scanned using the language
lexer, afterwards all `Other` tokens are lexed using the root
lexer.

The lexers from the `template` lexer package use this base lexer.

classes.name: tugboat._vendor.pygments.lexer.include
documentation: Indicates that a state should include rules from another state.
RegexLexer and ExtendedRegexLexer

classes.name: tugboat._vendor.pygments.lexer._inherit
documentation: Indicates the a state should inherit from its superclass.

classes.name: tugboat._vendor.pygments.lexer.combined
documentation: Indicates a state combined from multiple states.

classes.name: tugboat._vendor.pygments.lexer._PseudoMatch
documentation: A pseudo match object constructed from a string.

classes.name: tugboat._vendor.pygments.lexer._This
documentation:
Special singleton used for indicating the caller class.
Used by `using`.

classes.name: tugboat._vendor.pygments.lexer.default
documentation:
Indicates a state or state action (e.g. #pop) to apply.
For example default('#pop') is equivalent to ('', Token, '#pop')
Note that state tuples may be used as well.

:::{versionadded} 2.0
:::

classes.name: tugboat._vendor.pygments.lexer.words
documentation:
Indicates a list of literal words that is transformed into an optimized
regex that matches any of the words.

:::{versionadded} 2.0
:::

classes.name: tugboat._vendor.pygments.lexer.RegexLexerMeta

methods.name: tugboat._vendor.pygments.lexer.RegexLexerMeta._process_regex
documentation: Preprocess the regular expression component of a token definition.

methods.name: tugboat._vendor.pygments.lexer.RegexLexerMeta._process_token
documentation: Preprocess the token component of a token definition.

methods.name: tugboat._vendor.pygments.lexer.RegexLexerMeta._process_new_state
documentation: Preprocess the state transition action of a token definition.

methods.name: tugboat._vendor.pygments.lexer.RegexLexerMeta._process_state
documentation: Preprocess a single state definition.

methods.name: tugboat._vendor.pygments.lexer.RegexLexerMeta.process_tokendef
documentation: Preprocess a dictionary of token definitions.

methods.name: tugboat._vendor.pygments.lexer.RegexLexerMeta.get_tokendefs
documentation:
Merge tokens from superclasses in MRO order, returning a single tokendef
dictionary.

Any state that is not defined by a subclass will be inherited
automatically.  States that *are* defined by subclasses will, by
default, override that state in the superclass.  If a subclass wishes to
inherit definitions from a superclass, it can use the special value
"inherit", which will cause the superclass' state definition to be
included at that point in the state.

methods.name: tugboat._vendor.pygments.lexer.RegexLexerMeta.__call__
documentation: Instantiate cls after preprocessing its token definitions.

Metaclass for RegexLexer, creates the self._tokens attribute from
self.tokens on the first instantiation.

classes.name: tugboat._vendor.pygments.lexer.RegexLexer

methods.name: tugboat._vendor.pygments.lexer.RegexLexer.get_tokens_unprocessed
documentation:
Split `text` into (tokentype, text) pairs.

`stack` is the initial stack (default: `['root']`)

Base for simple stateful regular expression-based lexers.
Simplifies the lexing process so that you need only
provide a list of states and regular expressions.

classes.name: tugboat._vendor.pygments.lexer.LexerContext
documentation: A helper object that holds lexer position data.

classes.name: tugboat._vendor.pygments.lexer.ExtendedRegexLexer

methods.name: tugboat._vendor.pygments.lexer.ExtendedRegexLexer.get_tokens_unprocessed
documentation:
Split `text` into (tokentype, text) pairs.
If `context` is given, use this lexer context instead.
A RegexLexer that uses a context object to store its state.

classes.name: tugboat._vendor.pygments.lexer.ProfilingRegexLexerMeta
documentation: Metaclass for ProfilingRegexLexer, collects regex timing info.

classes.name: tugboat._vendor.pygments.lexer.ProfilingRegexLexer
